behavior_summary: U8P_SCROLL2::0080 (SCROLL2::0080) is the on-use handler for a 'Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals'. When a player reads the scroll it displays the scroll text, places the avatar in a short stasis, plays a sequence of visual and audio effects, then scans nearby world tiles/items for portal-like objects (doors, sealed walls, or items marked by quality/frame values).

For each candidate target the routine inspects item 'quality' and 'frame/shape' to decide if it is a sealed/portal object. If conditions indicate a dispellable portal it clears specific quality bits (Item::setQuality), spawns/dispatches a door/unseal routine (DOOR_NS::08B4) and plays temporary marker animations/sfx via helper spawns. If a target requires an additional component the script barks a failure message and shows a small effect.

If no matching targets are found the script barks an appropriate failure message ('Hmmmm... Nothing found.' or similar). Helper functions implement short-lived animated effect items and a separate pair of scroll behaviors for chest-trap detection/destruction (U8P_SCROLL2::0520 / U8P_SCROLL2::07F1 with U8P_SCROLL2::0755 as an inspector).
notable_control_flow: Start: perform initialization/delay (FREE::36E7) → show scroll text (process Scroll::read) → put avatar in stasis (setAvatarInStasis).
Animation phase: spawn several visual helpers oriented to caster (Npc::getDir → METHOD::15E7, FREE::1C19, FREE::29D8) and play sfx (playSFX) before scanning the area.
Scan phase: iterate nearby area/item slots (using a local item handle [BP-02h]) and for each candidate call item inspection logic (Item::getQuality, Item::getFrame, shape/frame tests).

Action on match: clear or modify item quality (Item::setQuality), spawn unseal/open routine (DOOR_NS::08B4), create temporary marker effects (Item::create, Item::pop), animate them (U8P_SCROLL2::042D / U8P_SCROLL2::047E) and then destroy markers (Item::destroy).
Failure reporting: if candidate needs another item/component bark via process Item::bark; if no candidates found bark an overall failure message. Return after clean up/animations complete.
open_questions: Exact bitmask values in Item::getQuality / Item::setQuality that identify 'portal' / 'sealed' / 'trap' states — code checks are present but mask semantics are inferred.
Precise shape/frame identifiers used to recognize each portal-like object or trap marker (the disassembly indicates comparisons but exact constants were not documented).
Area scanning radius and exact slots/items considered 'nearby' (loop indices and map geometry are inferred from iteration patterns but not fully specified).
Whether certain candidate types require a consumable component (the script barks about missing components) — the exact component identity and inventory interaction is not explicit.

main_function.name: U8P_SCROLL2::0080 (SCROLL2::0080)
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's use/reading of the Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals: present the scroll text and animation, then detect and attempt to dispel/unseal nearby portal-like objects with visual/audio feedback and failure messages when appropriate.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player reads/uses the dispelling scroll (Scroll::read event handler / item-use).
Triggered as an in-world process tied to the scroll item (pid and item pointer passed in).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::0080 (SCROLL2::0080)
function_map.role: Primary on-use handler for the dispelling scroll; coordinates UI, stasis, effects, scanning, target modification and feedback.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::042D
function_map.role: Animate a temporary effect item by advancing its frame index in a short loop with delays between frames.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::047E
function_map.role: Spawn a single visual/audio marker near a target (create/pop an item, playSFX, step frames, destroy) used when a portal is found or dispelled.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::0520
function_map.role: Behavior for a separate scroll variant: detection of trapped chests. Shows text/stasis/effects then scans for chests and dispatches inspectors (U8P_SCROLL2::0755).

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::0755
function_map.role: Inspect a chest/item for traps and, if found, create a temporary marker and return a small result indicating detection.

function_map.name: U8P_SCROLL2::07F1
function_map.role: Behavior for a destruction scroll variant: attempts to remove/destroy traps in nearby chests, providing feedback if none or no traps found.

function_map.name: FREE::36E7
function_map.role: Initialization/delay helper called at scroll start.

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::08B4
function_map.role: Spawned routine used to open/unseal a north-south door or door-like object after quality bits are cleared.

internal_calls.purpose: Animate temporary marker/effect frames associated with dispel animations.
Create and play a one-shot visual/audio marker near a candidate target when discovered or dispelled.
For each chest found, dispatch an inspector to check for traps and optionally spawn markers.
Use common inspection logic when trying to destroy traps in chests (re-uses detection internals).

external_calls.purpose: Initial short delay/synchronization at scroll use start (likely open/read animation staging).
Display the scroll text/description to the player and record the read action.
Temporarily prevent the avatar from acting/moving during the scroll animation/effects.
Obtain caster facing direction to orient spawned visual effects (METHOD::15E7).
Spawn oriented visual effects as part of the reading animation.
Spawned helper used repeatedly as a timed delay between animation frames or effect lifecycles.
Another short-lived visual helper spawned during the animation sequence.
Play sound effects tied to detection/dispelling and marker animations.
Read the target item's quality/flags to determine if it is sealed/portal/trapped.
Clear or update quality/flag bits to mark a portal as dispelled/unsealed.
Invoke door/unseal logic to open a north-south door after clearing quality flags.
Emit short textual feedback to the player (failure messages, notices that a component is required, or 'nothing found').
Create temporary marker/effect items used to mark detected objects.
Get target item's Z coordinate to position the marker correctly when popping it into the world.
Get target item's Y coordinate for marker placement.
Get target item's X coordinate for marker placement.
Place the created marker item into the world at computed coordinates.
Read current animation frame of the temporary effect item to step animation.
Advance the temporary effect item's animation frame during the short loop.
Insert a delay between animation frame steps by spawning a short-lived helper.
Clean up/destroy temporary marker/effect items after their animation completes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SCROLL2::0080 (SCROLL2::0080) is the on-use handler for a 'Scroll of Dispelling Magical Portals'. When a player reads the scroll it displays the scroll text, places the avatar in a short stasis, plays a sequence of visual and audio effects, then scans nearby world tiles/items for portal-like objects (doors, sealed walls, or items marked by quality/frame values).
